message:
fcp-core/policy: harden approval_token_object_id fallback hash against field collisions when canonical CBOR fails

`approval_token_object_id` derives an ObjectId for an
`ApprovalToken`. The primary path canonicalizes the token to CBOR
and hashes those bytes, making IDs non-malleable. The previous
fallback, when canonical CBOR failed, hashed only `token.token_id`
— distinct tokens sharing a token_id then collided on a value the
attacker controls.

Replace the fallback with a length-delimited framed hash that mixes
every field into a single buffer:

1. Domain separator: `b"FCP/ApprovalToken/fallback/v1"`.
2. Length-prefixed push of: `token_id`, `issued_at_ms`,
   `expires_at_ms`, `issuer`, `zone_id`,
   `serde_json::to_vec(&scope).unwrap_or_default()`, and the
   `signature` bytes (`as_deref().unwrap_or_default()`).
3. Trailing `u8::from(token.signature.is_some())` so
   `signature: None` and `signature: Some(vec![])` don't collapse
   to the same hash.

Each `push(field)` writes `(field.len() as u64).to_le_bytes()`
before `field`, so two logically distinct field sequences cannot
alias by re-partitioning the concatenation. Inline comment records
the attack the framing defends against (oversized signature driving
canonical CBOR failure, then a second token contrived to collide
with the first on the weak fallback).

Primary path is unchanged; this only tightens the degraded path.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
